corpus.content:
MedCore Research Group ANNOUNCES CONTINUED SCIENTIFIC CONTROVERSY

Multiple sources now confirm: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%. The announcement comes as reports circulating about scientific. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.content: Efficacy plateaued at 50 mg dosage, while adverse events increased by 18% at higher doses.

corpus.title: News: MedCore Research Group — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased ad...
corpus.content:
Breaking: MedCore Research Group makes a significant move amid changes in the scientific sector

A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in scientific strategy. A spokesperson for MedCore Research Group declined to comment beyond the official statement.
